title: Doellingeria obovata
taxonomy: Doellingeria obovata is a species of flowering plant in the aster family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Doellingeria and is a member of the tribe Astereae. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Asterales. Doellingeria obovata is native to the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada. This classification provides a clear understanding of the plant's taxonomic position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Doellingeria obovata, also known as Obovate Fleabane, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Doellingeria obovata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
